title: werauhia paupera
intro: Werauhia paupera, also known as the 'dwarf spike moss', is a rare and unusual plant species belonging to the family of Asplenioideae and is native to New Zealand. The medicinal properties of Werauhia paupera involve its traditional use in treating various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is said to possess health benefits such as anti-inflammatory properties and antiseptic qualities, making it useful for wound healing and soothing irritated skin. In traditional Maori medicine, the plant can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, often combined with other herbs, to create a tea or poultice for medicinal purposes.
